commonProblems.h2: Citroen Mehari Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Citroen Mehari's air-cooled 602cc engines (M28/1, A06/635, AK2) and modern E-Mehari electric drive units each have distinct failure patterns shaped by age, usage, and design. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement delivers better value for your specific Mehari variant.

problems.h4: Pushrod Tube Oil Leaks - Why Citroen Mehari 602cc Engines Lose Oil
problems.affectedModels: Citroen Mehari Standard 0.6, Club, Cassis, 4x4 (1968-1987, M28/1, A06/635, AK2)
problems.rootCause: Rubber pushrod tube seals harden and crack with age and heat cycles, allowing oil to seep onto hot cylinder heads and burn off.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove valve cover, replace pushrod tube seals and gaskets, reseal
repairOptions.longevity: Restores oil tightness; recommended at first sign of seepage or during routine service

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Seal replacement plus valve clearance adjustment, carburettor service, full gasket set
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses multiple age-related wear points; extends engine life 15-25k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with upgraded seals, pistons, bearings; bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when bottom-end wear or valve damage is present; like-new reliability

problems.recommendation: For early oil seepage, a specialist seal replacement is cost-effective and preserves originality. If blue smoke, low compression, or excessive tappet noise accompany the leak, a rebuilt 602cc unit with upgraded internals offers better long-term value on a Mehari worth £4,000-£12,000.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 602cc engine prices for your Citroen Mehari

problems.h4: Carburettor & Fuel System Failures - Why Citroen Mehari Engines Stall or Run Poorly
problems.affectedModels: Citroen Mehari all 602cc variants (1968-1987, M28/1, A06/635, AK2 with Solex carburettor)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Any mileage; age-related deterioration after 25+ years
problems.rootCause: Solex carburettor diaphragms, jets, and gaskets degrade; fuel contamination and ethanol in modern petrol accelerate wear and cause stalling or poor idle.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean, rebuild with seal kit, adjust idle/mixture; fuel filter replacement
repairOptions.longevity: Restores smooth running; recommended annually for stored classics

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Carb rebuild plus fuel pump inspection, lines replacement, tank clean if needed
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root causes of stalling/poor running; prevents repeat failures

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt engine with new carburettor or upgraded fuel system option; 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Ideal when carb issues mask deeper engine wear; eliminates fuel-related reliability concerns

problems.recommendation: Most Mehari running issues stem from the Solex carburettor rather than internal engine failure. Start with a specialist carb service - it's affordable and often resolves stalling, poor idle, or hesitation. Only consider engine replacement if compression tests reveal worn rings or valves.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Citroen Mehari 602cc engine replacement

problems.h4: Air-Cooled Engine Overheating - Why Citroen Mehari Engines Run Hot
problems.affectedModels: Citroen Mehari all 602cc variants (1968-1987, M28/1, A06/635, AK2)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Any mileage; triggered by component failure or sustained high-load driving
problems.rootCause: Cooling fan belt snap or slippage, clogged cooling fins, or incorrect ignition timing causing excessive heat in the air-cooled design.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace fan belt, clean cylinder head fins, check ignition timing
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents overheating; essential after long storage or before summer driving

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head removal, valve grind, new head gasket, compression check
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses warping or valve seat damage from moderate overheating events

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with upgraded cooling components; bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if overheating caused piston scuffing, bore damage, or cracked heads

problems.recommendation: Overheating on a Mehari is often preventable with routine belt checks and fin cleaning. If temperature spikes were brief and caught early, a cooling service plus compression test may suffice. Persistent overheating or loss of power signals internal damage - at that point, a rebuilt engine is more reliable than repeated repairs.
problems.cta: Compare Citroen Mehari rebuilt engine deals

problems.h4: Exhaust Valve Burn - Why Late Citroen Mehari Engines Lose Compression
problems.affectedModels: Citroen Mehari late-model 0.6, Azur Edition, Club Cassis (1975-1987, A06/635, AK2)
problems.rootCause: Lean fuel mixture, incorrect valve clearance, or prolonged high-RPM driving causes exhaust valves to overheat, burn, and lose sealing ability.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head removal, valve replacement, seat cutting, clearance reset
repairOptions.longevity: Restores compression and power; viable if block and pistons are sound

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with upgraded valve train, pistons, bearings; 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when valve failure has caused piston damage or when multiple wear items are due

problems.recommendation: A burnt exhaust valve often indicates broader wear in a high-mileage 602cc engine. If compression is low on multiple cylinders or oil consumption is high, investing in a full rebuilt unit with upgraded components delivers better reliability than a standalone valve job on a classic Mehari.
problems.cta: Get quotes for A06/635 Citroen Mehari engine replacement

problems.h4: E-Mehari Throttle Valve / Limp Mode - Why Electric Mehari Power Cuts Out
problems.rootCause: Throttle valve position sensor or accelerator pedal sensor fault triggers protective limp mode; MAF sensor contamination can compound the issue.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: OBD2 diagnostic, replace throttle position sensor or MAF sensor, software reset
repairOptions.longevity: Resolves intermittent power loss; recommended at first limp mode warning

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Drive Unit Control Module Service
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Module inspection, connector cleaning/replacement, wiring harness check, recalibration
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses electrical faults causing repeated limp mode; preserves original drive unit

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt E-Mehari Drive Unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt electric drive unit with upgraded sensors and control module; bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for persistent faults, high-voltage connection issues, or when original unit is beyond economical repair

problems.recommendation: E-Mehari limp mode is frequently sensor-related and can often be resolved with a specialist diagnostic and component replacement. However, if faults persist after sensor changes or high-voltage warnings appear, a rebuilt drive unit with upgraded electronics offers the most reliable long-term solution for this modern variant.
problems.cta: Compare E-Mehari electric motor replacement prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Citroen Mehari
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're dealing with classic 602cc oil leaks, carburettor issues, or modern E-Mehari sensor faults, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Citroen Mehari specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components targeting the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without paying main dealer premiums.

Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Citroen Mehari and engine code. engineSizes.h2: Citroen Mehari Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Citroen Mehari is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement unit.

items.title: Citroen Mehari 0.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 0.6L air-cooled petrol engine was the sole combustion powerplant fitted to the classic Citroen Mehari throughout its 1968-1987 production run. This simple, robust 602cc twin-cylinder unit, shared with the 2CV and Dyane, defined the Mehari's character with its distinctive sound and economical running, though its age means specialist knowledge is essential for replacement.
commonFailurePoints: exhaust valve burn at high mileage
items.cta: Compare Citroen Mehari 0.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Citroen Mehari Electric Drive Engines
items.description: The E-Mehari's electric drive unit represents the modern revival of the iconic nameplate, offering zero-emission urban mobility from 2016-2019. While mechanically simpler than the classic air-cooled engine, its electronic control systems require specialist diagnostics for faults like throttle valve defects or sensor-related limp mode. Not sure which engine size your Citroen Mehari needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. engineYears.h2: Citroen Mehari Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This technical timeline covers every major engine change across the Citroen Mehari's production from 1968 to 1987 and the E-Mehari revival 2016-2019, helping owners identify their exact engine code and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Mehari launched with the robust 602cc air-cooled M28/1 twin-cylinder engine, shared with the 2CV and Dyane, defining the model's character for two decades.
keyChanges: Mehari introduced to UK market with 602cc air-cooled petrol engine
M28/1 engine code established as standard fitment for all 2WD variants
Shared parts bin with 2CV6 and Dyane enables cross-model compatibility
Simple carburettor and ignition system designed for easy field maintenance
knownFor: Distinctive air-cooled twin-cylinder sound and economical running
Pushrod tube seals begin to harden after 20+ years, causing oil leaks
Solex carburettor requires periodic adjustment for smooth idle

years.description: Production matured with new trim levels and the introduction of the AK2 engine code designation for special editions and export builds.

AK2 engine code appears alongside M28/1 for certain export and limited editions
Minor updates to cooling fan belt routing and exhaust mounting points
Increased UK registration numbers as classic appeal grows

Exhaust valve burn reported on high-mileage units with lean carb settings

years.description: The updated A06/635 engine code replaced M28/1 for late-model production, featuring refined internals and improved carburettor calibration for emissions compliance.

Carburettor jetting adjusted for evolving fuel quality and emissions standards
Final production preparations for the rare 4x4 variant announced
knownFor: A06/635 shares core architecture with M28/1 but features updated component specs

Unique transfer case and drivetrain components not shared with 2WD models
Production ceased in 1983 due to limited commercial demand and complexity

faq.h2: Citroen Mehari Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Citroen Mehari engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: Citroen Mehari engine replacement costs typically range from £400 for a used 602cc unit to £2,900 for a fully rebuilt engine with upgraded internals. E-Mehari electric drive units cost £3,500-£5,800. Prices vary by engine code M28/1 or A06/635, variant 2WD or 4x4, and whether you choose supply-only or supply & fit.
keyPoints: Used 602cc engines start around £400-£1,200 supply-only from UK breakers
Rebuilt M28/1 or A06/635 engines range £1,400-£2,900 with 12-month warranty
E-Mehari electric motor replacement costs £3,500-£5,800 including diagnostics
Supply & fit labour adds £350-£650 depending on specialist location and variant
items.cta: Compare Citroen Mehari engine replacement prices from UK specialists now

items.question: What engine code is my Citroen Mehari - M28/1 or A06/635?
items.answer: Your Citroen Mehari engine code is stamped on the crankcase near the distributor. M28/1 fitted 1968-1979 standard 2WD models; A06/635 replaced it for 1979-1987 late models and the rare 4x4. AK2 is an alternative M28/1 designation for special editions. Correct identification ensures compatible replacement parts.
keyPoints: Locate the code on the crankcase left side near the distributor housing
M28/1 powered standard 2WD Meharis from launch in 1968 through 1979
A06/635 fitted late 2WD models 1979-1987 and all 4x4 variants exclusively
AK2 denotes export or special edition builds sharing M28/1 core architecture
items.cta: Get quotes for your Citroen Mehari M28/1 or A06/635 engine replacement

items.question: Can I fit a 2CV6 engine in my Citroen Mehari?
items.answer: Yes, Citroen Mehari and 2CV6 share the same 602cc air-cooled engine family, making direct swaps possible. Engine codes M28/1, A06/635 and AK2 are cross-compatible with minor mounting adjustments. This compatibility expands your options for affordable used or rebuilt engines from the wider 2CV/Dyane parts network across UK specialists.
keyPoints: Mehari and 2CV6 share identical 602cc air-cooled twin-cylinder engine architecture
M28/1, A06/635 and AK2 codes swap with minimal mounting bracket modifications
2CV/Dyane donor engines significantly increase available used and rebuilt inventory
UK specialists like The 2CV Shop supply compatible Mehari-ready 602cc units
items.cta: Compare Citroen Mehari compatible 2CV6 engine prices from UK suppliers

items.question: Is it worth rebuilding a Citroen Mehari 602cc engine?
items.answer: Rebuilding a Citroen Mehari 602cc engine is usually worth it for values over £4,000. A quality rebuild with upgraded pistons, rings and seals costs £1,400-£2,900 and restores original 28-29hp reliability. For lower-value projects or severe bottom-end damage, a used donor engine may offer better budget value.
keyPoints: Quality rebuilds cost £1,400-£2,900 with upgraded internals and 12-month warranty
Rebuilt engines restore original 28-29hp output with improved oil seal reliability
Used donor engines £400-£1,200 suit budget restorations with owner-led recommissioning
Consider vehicle value, intended use and availability of specialist rebuilders locally
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt Citroen Mehari 602cc engines with upgraded components

items.question: What causes oil leaks on the Citroen Mehari 602cc engine?
items.answer: Citroen Mehari 602cc oil leaks most commonly stem from hardened pushrod tube seals, valve cover gaskets or crankcase breather blockages. Age and heat cycles cause rubber seals to crack after 30+ years. Left unchecked, oil loss can lead to low oil pressure, increased wear and eventual engine seizure on high-mileage units.
keyPoints: Pushrod tube seals harden and crack with age, leaking oil onto hot heads
Valve cover gaskets and crankcase breathers also fail after decades of heat exposure
Regular inspection and seal replacement prevents oil loss and keeps engine clean
Use correct 20W-50 classic oil grade to maintain seal flexibility and pressure
items.cta: Compare Citroen Mehari 602cc engine rebuild prices addressing oil leak failures

items.question: Why does my E-Mehari go into limp mode?
items.answer: E-Mehari limp mode is typically triggered by throttle valve position sensor faults, MAF sensor contamination, or high-voltage connection issues. These electronic faults cause the control module to restrict power for safety. Diagnostic scanning identifies the specific fault code, and specialist replacement of the sensor or drive unit component usually resolves the issue.
keyPoints: Throttle valve position sensor failure is the most documented cause of E-Mehari limp mode
MAF sensor contamination from dust or moisture can trigger reduced power warnings
High-voltage connector inspection is essential during any E-Mehari motor replacement enquiry
Specialist diagnostic equipment is required to read E-Mehari specific fault codes accurately
items.cta: Enquire about Citroen E-Mehari electric motor replacement from UK specialists

items.question: How long does a Citroen Mehari 602cc engine last?
items.answer: A well-maintained Citroen Mehari 602cc engine can exceed 100,000 miles with regular valve adjustments, oil changes and seal replacements. Most classic units today show wear after 30+ years rather than mileage. Rebuilt engines with upgraded pistons, rings and seals typically deliver another 50,000+ miles of reliable classic motoring when maintained correctly.
keyPoints: Original 602cc engines often exceed 100,000 miles with proper valve and oil maintenance
Age-related seal deterioration is now the primary failure mode versus pure mileage wear
Rebuilt units with upgraded internals typically deliver 50,000+ additional reliable miles
Regular 3,000-mile oil changes with 20W-50 grade preserve bottom-end longevity
items.cta: Compare Citroen Mehari 602cc rebuilt engine prices for long-term reliability

items.question: What oil should I use in my Citroen Mehari 602cc engine?
items.answer: Use 20W-50 mineral oil formulated for classic air-cooled engines in your Citroen Mehari 602cc. This grade maintains film strength at operating temperature and keeps older seals flexible. Change oil every 3,000 miles or annually. Avoid modern low-viscosity synthetics which can increase pushrod tube seal leaks on aged engines.
keyPoints: 20W-50 mineral oil maintains pressure and seal flexibility in aged 602cc engines
Change oil every 3,000 miles or annually whichever comes first for classic use
Avoid modern 5W-30 synthetics which can exacerbate pushrod tube seal leaks
UK specialists like The 2CV Shop supply recommended classic oil grades
items.cta: Get quotes for Citroen Mehari engine replacement with correct oil specification guidance

items.question: Does the Citroen Mehari 4x4 use a different engine?
items.answer: The Citroen Mehari 4x4 uses the same 602cc air-cooled engine family but exclusively with the A06/635 code. Core architecture matches 2WD M28/1 units, but 4x4 models feature reinforced mounting points and different ancillary routing for the transfer case. Replacement engines must match A06/635 specifications for correct fitment.
keyPoints: Mehari 4x4 exclusively fitted with A06/635 engine code from 1980-1983 production
Core 602cc architecture matches 2WD units but with reinforced mounting bracketry
Transfer case integration requires specific ancillary routing and exhaust clearance checks
Only 1,200 units produced making A06/635 4x4 engines rare and valuable
items.cta: Enquire about Citroen Mehari 4x4 A06/635 engine availability from UK specialists
